message:
fix: Bounds validation for tokens and negative buffer coordinates

Adds defensive checks to prevent panics and undefined behavior from
malformed data or edge-case coordinates.

**Highlighted buffer token validation (src/highlight/highlighted_buffer.rs):**
- Add bounds checking in `styled_line()` and `highlighted_line_segments()`
- Skip tokens where `start > end` (inverted bounds)
- Skip tokens where `end > line_byte_len` (exceeds line length)
- Prevents panics when tokenizers produce malformed tokens
- Added `MalformedTokenizer` test mock and test coverage

**Buffer draw region negative coordinate fix (src/buffer/mod.rs):**
- Fix potential issue in `draw_buffer_region()` when x or y are very
  negative (e.g., -20 with width 10)
- Added `.max(0)` before casting to u32 to ensure `dest_x_end` and
  `dest_y_end` don't wrap around
- Prevents hang/crash when drawing buffers far off-screen
- Added test `test_draw_buffer_negative_out_of_bounds`

**Why this matters:**
- External tokenizers may produce invalid token spans
- Scroll/pan operations may produce large negative coordinates
- Both could cause panics or infinite loops without validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
